name: Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268)
name_original: Bosque de Las Morillas – circular desde Alfarnate (PR-A 268)
region_intro: Jogging around Alfarnate offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its mountainous terrain and forested areas. The region features a mix of challenging ascents and more gentle paths through natural parks. Runners can explore trails that wind through hills and valleys, providing varied elevation changes. The area's natural beauty includes dense woodlands and open vistas, making it suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Alfarnate The most popular jogging route is Sierra de San Jorge Trail (PR-A 353), a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268), a moderate 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This running route winds through a scenic forest, offering a more sheltered experience. Local runners also love the Impressive Gorge loop from Villanueva del Rosario, a 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail leading through a dramatic gorge landscape, often completed in about 5 hours. Jogging around Alfarnate is defined by mountain trails, forest paths, and scenic gorges. The network offers options for all ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more challenging running trails. The routes in Alfarnate are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from 5 reviews. Nearly 200 runners have used komoot to explore Alfarnate's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Alfarnate?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Alfarnate listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences for all levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Alfarnate?
A: Yes, Alfarnate offers several routes suitable for beginners. While many trails feature mountainous terrain, you can find easier options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996265302/forest-of-las-morillas-circular-from-alfarnate-pr-a-268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.6-mile path that provides a more sheltered and accessible experience through scenic woodlands.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for running trails in the Alfarnate area?
A: Running routes around Alfarnate vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter paths like the 3.6-mile &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996265302/forest-of-las-morillas-circular-from-alfarnate-pr-a-268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268)&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 45 minutes. Longer, more challenging trails, such as the 18.5-mile &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Impressive Gorge loop from Villanueva del Rosario&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Alfarnate?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Alfarnate are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996265302/forest-of-las-morillas-circular-from-alfarnate-pr-a-268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop through the forest.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging routes around Alfarnate?
A: The jogging routes around Alfarnate offer diverse scenery, from mountainous terrain with panoramic views to dense woodlands and dramatic gorges. You'll encounter natural beauty like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1116374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafarraya Plain&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Gomer and Doña Ana cliffs&lt;/a&gt;, providing stunning vistas during your run.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks to see along the running trails?
A: Yes, several running routes pass by interesting natural monuments and historical sites. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Vilo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;. For a historical touch, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31438192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moorish Archway of Alfarnate loop from Alfarnate&lt;/a&gt; takes you past a notable local landmark.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Alfarnate?
A: The running trails in Alfarnate are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars from numerous reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents, and the beautiful natural surroundings, highlighting the region's suitability for different running preferences.

Q: Is Alfarnate a good destination for family-friendly running or jogging?
A: While many routes in Alfarnate involve mountainous terrain, there are options suitable for families looking for a less strenuous jog. Look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' with less elevation gain, such as sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996265302/forest-of-las-morillas-circular-from-alfarnate-pr-a-268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a pleasant, sheltered environment.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails around Alfarnate?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the natural trails around Alfarnate, especially in less populated areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in nature parks or near livestock, and to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Alfarnate?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for jogging in Alfarnate, offering mild temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable, focusing on shaded routes like those in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996265302/forest-of-las-morillas-circular-from-alfarnate-pr-a-268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of Las Morillas&lt;/a&gt;. Winters are typically mild, but higher elevations might experience cooler temperatures.

Q: Where can I park when accessing jogging trails near Alfarnate?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the village of Alfarnate, often close to trailheads or public spaces. For routes starting from nearby villages like Villanueva del Rosario, you'll typically find parking within the village itself, allowing convenient access to trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de los Becerros loop from Villanueva del Rosario&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the running routes?
A: Alfarnate village itself offers several cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your run. For routes that venture further out, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, as amenities might be scarce directly along the trails.
tips: From Cerro Olmedo there are beautiful views of the Vilo peak and the landscape that surrounds it. The circular route through the Bosque de las Morillas is an entertaining itinerary without difficulty.
